summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that a malicious JavaScript file placed at ~/.marked.js is automatically loaded and executed when the marked CLI runs, achieving arbitrary code execution without user consent.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should demonstrate that a malicious config file in ~/.marked.js is automatically loaded and executed when the marked CLI is invoked, creating a proof-of-concept file and demonstrating arbitrary code execution.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh successfully built the marked library, installed the CLI globally, placed a malicious config file in a fake home directory, and executed the marked CLI. The malicious config was automatically loaded and executed, creating a proof file at /tmp/marked-cli-poc-executed.txt and logging attacker-controlled messages to the console.
verification_method: The test places a malicious config file at ~/.marked.js that creates a proof file when executed. After running the marked CLI, the test checks for the existence and content of the proof file to verify that arbitrary code execution occurred.
evidence_produced_by_run: Proof file /tmp/marked-cli-poc-executed.txt was created by malicious config
Console output shows [ATTACKER CODE EXECUTED] messages from malicious config
Malicious config received the marked object and could manipulate parser behavior
No user confirmation or opt-in was required - config loaded automatically
Execution occurred with full user privileges in the container

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
